title: Bronco Racing FSAE
subtitle: High-voltage tractive systems for SCU's Formula SAE electric cars
role: HV & Co-Electrical Lead (BR1) · HV Engineer (BR2, current)
pitch: End-to-end ownership of the >400 V electrical stack across two car generations: BMS, charging, safety circuits, CAN, and thermal management.
highlights: Programmed the Orion BMS 2 on a ~420 V max accumulator: cell monitoring, SoC, safety interlocks
Designed and reviewed shutdown, precharge, discharge, and IMD PCBs; caught protection-logic errors before fab
540 V on-board charger install plus a charging cart with safety shutdown and deadman lock
140+ thermistor harness with ~50 percent measurement-accuracy improvement
Architected the vehicle CAN network with a custom Python diagnostic GUI
Now owning BMS, thermal, charging, DAQ, driver display, and LV for the 2027 car

blocks.text: BR1 (2026 car): the HV stack, end to end

blocks.md: As High-Voltage & Co-Electrical Lead I owned the tractive-system electrical stack: programming and configuring the Orion BMS 2 for the accumulator, designing thermal-management expansion modules, programming and installing a 540 V on-board charger, and designing the charging cart with current/voltage control, a safety-shutdown circuit, and a deadman lock. I designed schematic sections for the shutdown and charging-shutdown circuits, reviewed the precharge, discharge, and IMD boards for FSAE EV compliance, and contributed to the tractive-system layout across the vehicle. The 2026 season ended with the HV system bench-validated; the team made a deliberate safety call not to run it at competition.

rows.label: BR1 accumulator
rows.value: 100s3p, Molicel P45B, ~360 V nominal / ~420 V max

rows.label: BMS
rows.value: Orion BMS 2, custom thermal expansion modules

rows.label: Charging
rows.value: 540 V on-board charger + custom charging cart with safety shutdown and deadman lock

rows.label: Comms
rows.value: Vehicle CAN network, STM32 firmware, Python diagnostic GUI

blocks.md: For the 2027 car I'm the HV Engineer across electrical and powertrain: all powertrain electronics for an Emrax 228 motor with a DTI controller, plus the BMS, thermal management, charging system, data acquisition, driver display, and all low-voltage electronics. I'm currently building the charging cart from scratch: rebuilding the shutdown-circuit PCB, soldering, wiring, system architecture, CAD and 3D printing, and documentation.

rows.label: BR2 pack
rows.value: ~120s4p, Molicel P50B 21700, ~432 V nominal / 504 V max, ~8.6 kWh

rows.label: My scope
rows.value: BMS, thermal, charging, DAQ, driver display, LV electronics, charging cart

blocks.heading: Battery-safety innovation concept (in exploration)
items: Evaluating DEP BRT in-cell thermal-runaway sensors that detect electrochemical change before venting; the vendor's validation cites roughly 12 minutes of pre-vent warning.
Planned as an FSAE innovation-award concept submission, not a delivered result.
